Terms of Use

Career Solutions for Today · Resume Portfolio · Version 1.0 · Last Updated: April 2026

1. Acceptance of Terms

By using this service, you agree to these Terms. If you do not agree, do not use the platform.

2. Description of Service

This platform provides:

  • Resume creation and editing tools
  • AI-powered resume enhancements
  • Career planning tools (including 90-day plans)
  • Export and sharing features

3. User Responsibilities

You agree to:

  • Provide accurate information
  • Use the service lawfully
  • Not upload harmful, illegal, or misleading content
  • Review AI-generated content before using it

You are fully responsible for your resume content.

4. AI Disclaimer

AI-generated content:

  • May not be accurate or complete
  • Should be reviewed before use
  • Does not guarantee job outcomes

We are not responsible for decisions made based on AI output.

5. Accounts

You are responsible for maintaining account security and all activity under your account. We may suspend or terminate accounts for misuse.

6. Subscriptions & Billing

Some features require a paid subscription. Subscriptions are billed on a recurring basis (e.g., monthly). Billing is processed securely via Stripe.

By subscribing, you agree to:

  • Automatic renewal unless canceled
  • Charges at the stated billing interval

7. Cancellation

You may cancel your subscription at any time.

  • Cancellation stops future billing
  • Access remains until the end of the billing period

To cancel your Stripe subscription: email stevenk@careersolutionsfortoday.com or use the Manage Subscription option in your account menu.

Stripe does not manage cancellations on your behalf.

8. Refund Policy

All payments are non-refundable unless otherwise stated. We do not provide prorated refunds for partial billing periods.

9. Intellectual Property

You retain ownership of your resume content. We retain ownership of the platform, design, and technology. You grant us a limited license to store, process, and display your content to provide the service.

10. Acceptable Use

You may not:

  • Use the service for unlawful purposes
  • Attempt to reverse engineer or exploit the platform
  • Abuse AI features or automate misuse
  • Upload malicious or harmful content

11. Termination

We may suspend or terminate access if you violate these Terms.

12. Limitation of Liability

We are not liable for job outcomes or hiring decisions, errors in resumes or AI-generated content, or loss of data or interruptions. Use the service at your own risk.

13. Changes to Terms

We may update these Terms at any time. Continued use means acceptance of updates.

14. Governing Law

These Terms are governed by the laws of the State of Arizona, without regard to conflict of law principles.

15. Contact

stevenk@careersolutionsfortoday.com

Privacy Policy

Career Solutions for Today · Resume Portfolio · Version 1.0 · Last Updated: April 2026

1. Information We Collect

We collect information you provide directly when using Resume Portfolio, including:

  • Name, email address, and account details
  • Resume content, work history, and career-related data
  • Uploaded files (e.g., resumes, documents, images)
  • 90-day plans and other generated content
  • Payment-related metadata (processed securely via third parties)

We also automatically collect:

  • Device and browser information
  • IP address
  • Usage data (pages visited, actions taken)

2. How We Use Your Information

  • Provide and operate Resume Portfolio
  • Save and manage your resumes and career plans
  • Generate AI-powered suggestions and improvements
  • Process subscriptions and manage billing
  • Improve platform performance and user experience
  • Communicate important updates or support responses

3. AI Processing (OpenAI)

We use OpenAI API services to process certain resume content. We do not use customer content to train our models by default through the API, but data may be retained temporarily by service providers for abuse prevention, security, legal compliance, or operational reasons.

You should avoid entering highly sensitive personal information (Social Security numbers, financial details, etc.).

4. Camera & Device Permissions

The Career Solutions for Today mobile app may request access to your device's camera. Camera access is used solely to allow you to take or select a profile photo for your resume. We do not record, stream, or store camera data beyond the photo you choose to upload. Camera access is optional — you can skip photo upload and still use all core features of the app.

5. Data Storage & Security

Your data is stored using secure cloud services, including:

  • Firebase Authentication
  • Firestore (database)
  • Firebase Storage

We implement reasonable safeguards to protect your information, but no system is 100% secure.

6. Data Retention

We retain your data as long as your account is active, as needed to provide services, and as required for legal, tax, or billing obligations. Deleted content may remain in backups for a limited period.

7. Account & Data Deletion

You may delete your account and all associated data — including saved resumes, career plans, profile information, and uploaded files — directly from the account menu using the "Delete Account & Data" option. Account deletion requires that any active subscription be canceled first. Deleted data is permanently removed from our active systems; however, residual copies may persist in backups for a limited period and data required for legal, tax, or billing obligations may be retained as permitted by law.

If you no longer have access to the app, you can also request deletion via our public page: Delete Account & Data.

8. Your Rights

You may:

  • Access your data
  • Update or correct your information
  • Delete your account and associated data
  • Request a copy of your data

To make a request, contact: stevenk@careersolutionsfortoday.com

9. Cookies & Tracking

We may use cookies or similar technologies to maintain sessions, improve functionality, and analyze usage. You can control cookies through your browser settings.

10. Third-Party Services

We use trusted third-party providers, including:

  • Stripe — payment processing (Stripe Privacy)
  • Firebase (Google) — authentication, database, storage (Firebase Privacy)
  • OpenAI — AI processing (OpenAI Privacy)

These providers have their own privacy policies.

11. Data Sharing

We do not sell your personal data. We only share data with service providers needed to operate the platform, when required by law, or to protect rights and prevent fraud.

12. Children's Privacy

This service is not intended for users under 18.

13. Changes to This Policy

We may update this policy. Continued use means you accept the updated terms.

14. Contact

stevenk@careersolutionsfortoday.com

How AI is Used

Career Solutions for Today · Resume Portfolio · Plain-English explanation of every AI step in this app.

1. What we use AI for

Resume Portfolio uses OpenAI’s API (currently the gpt-4o family) for a small set of focused tasks:

  • Resume extraction — turning an uploaded PDF/DOCX/TXT or pasted text into structured fields (name, headline, experience, education, skills, etc.).
  • Job posting import — reading a LinkedIn or pasted job description and extracting role title, company, requirements, and keywords.
  • Role research — suggesting role-specific skills, themes, and KPIs to consider when targeting a position.
  • Cover letter drafting — producing a first-pass cover letter based on your resume and the target role.
  • Resume / plan optimization — comparing your resume against the target role and suggesting rewrites for the headline, summary, experience bullets, role summaries, cover letter paragraphs, and 90-day / 12-month / 2-year plan phases.
  • Interview prep — generating practice behavioral, technical, and “questions to ask the employer” lists for the target role.

That’s the entire list. We do not use AI to make hiring decisions, score you as a candidate, or judge your suitability for any job.

2. What data is sent to OpenAI

Only the data needed for the task you triggered is sent. Nothing is sent in the background, on page load, or while you’re just typing.

  • For extraction: the raw text we pulled from your uploaded file (or the text you pasted) plus a short instruction telling the model to return JSON.
  • For optimization: the structured resume fields you see on screen (headline, summary, skills, experience bullets, education, plus your cover letter and plan if you generated them) and the target role details (title, company, description, keywords).
  • For interview prep / role research: the target role title, company, and a compact summary of your background.

We do not send: your password, payment information, billing data, IP address, browsing history outside this app, or any data from other tabs/sites. We do not send your account email or Firebase user ID to OpenAI — only the resume and role content itself.

Important: avoid putting highly sensitive personal data (Social Security numbers, full date of birth, financial account numbers, government IDs, medical details) into your resume or pasted text. Anything in those fields will be sent to the AI provider as part of the request.

3. What we get back

Every AI request is constrained to return structured JSON, not free-form text, so we can validate it before showing it to you. Typical responses include:

  • Extraction: a normalized resume object (name, headline, sections, experience array, etc.).
  • Optimization:
    • A matchScore with an overall 0–100 number, label (e.g. “Strong Match”), and explanation.
    • A baselineScore (your resume as-is) and a projectedScore (estimated score after suggested changes).
    • A scoreGate object that tells the app whether the changes would improve, maintain, or hurt the match.
    • A suggestions object with rewrite candidates for the resume, cover letter, and plan. Each rewrite carries the original text, the suggested text, a short reason, and a safeToApply flag.
    • Optional resumeInsights, atsCheck, and interview_prep data.

4. How “Apply Safe Updates” decides what to apply

When you click Apply Safe Updates, the app does not blindly accept everything the AI suggested. Each suggested change is checked against three rules first:

  1. Score gate — if the AI’s own scoreGate.status is rejected (i.e. the changes would lower your match score), nothing is applied. You’ll see a banner explaining why.
  2. Per-item safety flag — the AI marks each suggestion with safeToApply. Items flagged false are skipped.
  3. Placeholder check — the app scans every suggestion for placeholder tokens like [insert measurable result], [X%], [$X], or [N-person team]. If any are found, that item is shown with a Review only badge and is not applied automatically — you decide whether to fill in the real number and apply it manually.

Items that pass all three checks get the Will apply / Applied badge in the Optimize results view, so you can see exactly what changed before and after you click apply.

The Skills to add section is intentionally review only. We never auto-merge skills into your profile because you’re the only one who knows whether a suggested skill is actually accurate for you.

5. Snapshots, undo, and your control

Before applying any AI suggestions, the app saves a snapshot of your resume, cover letter, and plan. You can:

  • Click Undo at any time to roll back to the snapshot.
  • Edit any field manually after applying — nothing is locked.
  • Re-run optimization as many times as you want.
  • Skip optimization entirely and use the builder as a regular resume editor.

6. Server-side timeouts and error handling

AI calls are made from our backend server, not directly from your browser, so your API key and request shape are never exposed. Each call has a hard timeout (about 60 seconds) and the server returns a clear error to the app if the model is slow or returns malformed output. When that happens, your existing data is left untouched.

7. Training and retention

We use OpenAI’s API (not ChatGPT). Per OpenAI’s API policy, content sent through the API is not used to train OpenAI’s models by default. OpenAI may retain API request data for a limited period for abuse monitoring and legal compliance. See OpenAI’s privacy policy for details.

On our side, we store the results of optimization (your suggestions, scores, applied changes) alongside your resume version in Firestore so you can come back to them later. We do not log or store the raw prompt text after the response is returned.

8. AI is a draft, not the final word

Every AI suggestion is a starting point. Match scores are estimates, not a guarantee of being interviewed or hired. Always read the rewrites for accuracy, factual correctness, and your own voice before applying them or sending the final document.

9. Questions

If you have questions about how AI is used in this app, contact: stevenk@careersolutionsfortoday.com.
